title: Enter docs from homepage via primary navigation
description: Verifies a first-time visitor can scroll the homepage, see component preview cards rendered, and enter the docs landing page via primary navigation.

steps.description: Navigate to /
Scroll to the marketing sections below the hero
Scroll to the section containing embedded component previews
Verify embedded component preview cards are displayed
Click the primary navigation link to open documentation
Verify the documentation page is displayed

title: llms.txt renders as plain text and is non-empty
description: Verifies a tooling client can open /llms.txt and observe non-empty plain-text content suitable for ingestion.

steps.description: Navigate to /llms.txt
Verify the page displays raw text content rather than a documentation layout
Verify the text content is non-empty

title: Deep-link to theming docs and return to homepage via header
description: Verifies a developer can open the theming doc directly, see MDX rendered with sidebar context, then return to the homepage via header navigation and re-enter docs.

steps.description: Navigate to /docs/theming
Verify documentation content is rendered
Verify the docs navigation sidebar is displayed
Click the header navigation link to go to the homepage
Click the header navigation link to open documentation
Verify the documentation page is displayed

title: llms-full.txt renders a larger non-HTML text export
description: Verifies a tooling client can open /llms-full.txt and observe a larger non-empty text export without HTML framing.

steps.description: Navigate to /llms-full.txt
Verify the page displays raw text content rather than a documentation layout
Verify the text content is non-empty

title: Cross-navigate from homepage into installation docs
description: Verifies a visitor can start on the homepage, enter docs, and reach the installation page through normal in-app navigation.

steps.description: Navigate to /
Scroll to the hero section CTAs
Click the call-to-action that enters documentation
Click the link to open the installation guide from the docs entry view
Verify the installation documentation content is displayed
Verify the docs navigation sidebar is displayed

title: Read registry documentation via direct deep link
description: Verifies a returning developer can open the registry documentation directly and see the docs shell and MDX content rendered without issues.

steps.description: Navigate to /docs/registry
Verify documentation content is rendered
Verify the docs navigation sidebar is displayed

title: Copy-page control produces non-empty copied content
description: Verifies a developer can use the copy-page control on a representative doc page and the copied result is non-empty when pasted into an in-page field.

steps.description: Navigate to /docs/installation
Scroll through the page to ensure the main content is present
Click the copy-page control
Focus a text input or text area on the page if available for pasting
Press Ctrl+V to paste clipboard contents
Verify the pasted content is non-empty
Verify the pasted content includes multiple lines of text

title: Docs open reliably from homepage header after scrolling
description: Verifies that after scrolling the homepage, the header navigation still works to open docs and render content correctly.

steps.description: Navigate to /
Scroll to mid-page marketing sections
Click the header navigation link to open documentation
Verify the documentation page is displayed
Verify documentation content is rendered

title: Homepage sections are readable and stable after scrolling
description: Verifies the homepage hero and key marketing sections remain usable when a visitor scrolls through the page and back to the top.

steps.description: Navigate to /
Scroll to the bottom of the page
Scroll back to the top of the page
Verify the hero section content is displayed
Verify embedded component preview cards are displayed

title: Docs table of contents is absent or minimal on short pages without breaking layout
description: Verifies that on a short documentation page (e.g. MCP docs), the absence or minimal presence of a table of contents does not prevent reading the content or using the sidebar. Changelog is intentionally excluded from navigation.

steps.description: Navigate to /docs/mcp
Scroll through the page content
Verify documentation content is rendered
Verify the docs navigation sidebar is displayed

title: Per-page MDX markdown route returns raw markdown for docs
description: Verifies the /llms.mdx/docs/.../content.md route serves processed markdown (text/markdown) for a representative docs page, matching the MDX-backed docs content.

steps.description: Perform a GET request to /llms.mdx/docs/installation/content.md (or another stable docs slug with content.md suffix)
Verify the response Content-Type indicates markdown or plain text (e.g. text/markdown)
Verify the response body is non-empty and includes a markdown heading (e.g. starts with #)
Verify the body content aligns with the same page title or topic as the HTML docs route for that slug

title: Terminal theme selector updates the in-browser terminal renderer
description: Verifies changing the terminal theme in the docs preview chrome updates the terminal preview (e.g. background/foreground tokens applied via terminalThemeKey), not only the select label.

steps.description: Navigate to /docs/theming where the theme showcase preview is present
Locate the terminal theme dropdown (NativeSelect) in the preview chrome
Note the current computed background or canvas color on the terminal preview container (.terminal-preview-root or xterm canvas)
Select a different terminal theme from the dropdown (e.g. switch from default to Dracula or Nord)
Verify the terminal preview background or xterm theme colors change to match the selected palette
Verify the preview remains interactive/visible after the theme change (no blank terminal)

title: Search: Meta+C copies install command and Enter/click navigates
description: Verifies the command menu highlights a registry item; ⌘+C / Ctrl+C copies the shadcn add command. There is no in-UI copy confirmation (the dialog closes on copy)—assert success via navigator.clipboard.readText() after the shortcut. Separately, open search again and select the same result (Enter or click) and assert navigation to the component docs URL.

steps.description: Navigate to /docs
Grant clipboard-read permission for the app origin (Playwright: context.grant_permissions) so tests can read navigator.clipboard
Open the documentation search dialog (⌘K or Ctrl+K or click Search)
Search for a known component (e.g. badge) and ensure a registry-backed result is highlighted/first so the internal copy payload is set
Press Meta+C (macOS) or Ctrl+C (Windows/Linux) to copy (expect the modal to close; do not rely on visible “Copied” UI)
Read the clipboard via the browser Clipboard API (e.g. page.evaluate(() => navigator.clipboard.readText())) and verify it contains the install command with shadcn and the registry slug
Open search again, find the same component, and activate the result with Enter or click (not Meta+C)
Verify the browser URL matches the component docs path (e.g. /docs/components/badge) and the page loads

title: Analytics events fire for tracked copy actions
description: Verifies Vercel Analytics receives custom track() events when copying the install command from search. Intercept POST `/_vercel/insights/event` (application/json). Payload uses `en` for the event name (`copy_npm_command`) and `ed.code` for the full install line (e.g. `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @termcn/...`), plus `o`, `sdkn`, `sdkv`, `ts`, etc.

steps.description: Register a request listener that collects POST requests to `/_vercel/insights/event` (same-origin as the app)
Navigate to the site, open search, highlight a registry item with an install command, and trigger copy with Meta+C or Ctrl+C
Verify at least one POST to `/_vercel/insights/event` was sent after copy
Parse JSON: `en` is `copy_npm_command`, `ed.code` is a non-empty install command string containing `shadcn` (and typically `dlx`/`pnpm`/`npx`)
